categories.rationale: GSD Core has explicit runtime, command, skill, workflow, state, capability, installer, and adapter boundaries with generated-drift checks. Its size and multiple compatibility seams add complexity, and no independent architecture review was found.

[UNVERIFIED] Closed command checks, generated-sync guards, security/install/integration suites, property tests, coverage floors, and a multi-OS matrix are present, but this audit did not rerun the full Node matrix.
Hierarchical planning, research, plan checking, execution waves, review, verification, UAT, and milestone closure form a deep method. Independent outcome studies remain absent.
Persistent state, plan checkers, drift gates, verification debt, phase transitions, and atomic commits create a strong lifecycle. It is not bound to Loom's exact causal-credit and G1 contract.

Profiles, quick/full modes, workspaces, specialist flows, and capabilities adapt broadly across software development. An explicit authority-gated unknown non-software domain discovery flow was not found.

Fresh subagent contexts, namespace meta-skills, headroom tracking, and measured skill-byte budgets directly address context cost. The docs call headroom heuristic, and complete provider token/cache/retry receipts are absent.
[UNVERIFIED] A quick path removes optional phase overhead, but no timing run or provider-attested production p95 corpus was obtained; the command ecosystem remains large.
Namespace meta-skills reduce eager skill listings, but users still navigate dozens of distinct commands. This is capable rather than minimal.

Consent-gated session profiling, questionnaire fallback, context-dependent splits, phase learning extraction, and HITL graduation are real. They are explicit workflows, not automatic bounded effect attribution with forgetting and measured benefit.

Project learnings and profiles are scoped, and an optional local-first memory capability is designed. The core does not provide Loom's encrypted owner vault, deterministic device merge, bounded active memory, or deletion-floor semantics.

The active successor supports a very broad set of runtimes with installers, generated registries, hooks, and adapter-specific guidance. A full independent live-host reproduction was not performed here.

[UNVERIFIED] Security scans, mutation tests, install tests, drift checks, state locks, and defensive hooks are extensive, but crash, power-loss, replay, and hostile-host containment were not reproduced.
GSD states that profiling stays local, ships no usage telemetry today, and keeps audit arguments opt-in. It lacks an all-file public-cut firewall and encrypted sovereign owner-vault proof.

Unit, integration, install, security, slow, coverage, property, and mutation suites combine with multi-OS CI and npm OIDC provenance. Deterministic clean-room release reproduction was not established, so this is not 100.
Architecture, features, configuration, commands, how-to guides, references, and translations are extensive. The system's breadth raises onboarding cost and no independent study was found.
Generated capability registries, state files, health checks, and opt-in dispatch auditing provide strong visibility. Provider-bound usage receipts and an independent claim audit are absent.
The active successor is public and the archived predecessor redirects users to it, with substantial combined adoption. No independent user study or hostile audit satisfying this rubric was found.
